task.title: Using separate CICS translation for COBOL with CICS Transaction Server for OS/390
task.shortdesc: You can use CICS Transaction Server for OS/390 to provide translation support for EXEC CICS statements. Using separate CICS translation causes CICS translation to occur as a separate step from the compilation step. The translation results are handed off to your compiler.

keywords.indexterm: COBOLCICS Transaction Server for OS/390Using separate CICS translation

context.p: Use integrated CICS translation whenever possible, but you might need to use separate CICS translation under the following circumstances:

ul.li: You prefer to use your own tested and stable JCL to compile your code
Your compiler does not support integrated CICS translation

p.note: To perform syntax checks on your code, you must use integrated CICS translation.
The syntax check feature does not support separate CICS translation.

steps.stepsection: To enable separate CICS translation support for COBOL using CICS Transaction Server for OS/390, follow these steps:

step.cmd: Select the resource and then select Property GroupEdit Associated Property Group
To open the COBOL Settings page, click the COBOL tab.

step.stepresult: This action activates the list beside the checkbox.

step.cmd: From the list, select the level of CICS installed on the remote system.

step.stepresult: The CICS Translator Step Options area opens.

step.cmd: Complete the following fields and save the settings:

dlentry.dd: Options to be set for your CICS translator. Leave this field blank to use the default of options.
The location of your system library (SYSLIB). You must specify this location so that your CICS translator can find your copy members.

step.stepresult: The Link Step Options window opens.

step.cmd: Complete the following fields and click OK:

dlentry.dd: Libraries to link your code with. For example, you might specify CICSVS.TS230.CICS.SDFHLOAD and CEEV2R10.SCEELKED in this field.

Select this option and enter the following link-edit control cards in the field:
INCLUDE SYSLIB(DFHELII)
INCLUDE SYSLIB(EQUDCCXT)
ORDER CEESTART
The data set where your load module must be placed after the link step. For example, you might set this location to the DFHRPL concatenation of your CICS region.

step.cmd: Close and save the property group.

taskbody.postreq: You can now run remote syntax checks and perform remote builds on your configured resource. You can specify more copy libraries, compiler options, and JCL in the compiler properties pane by clicking + to expand ELAXFCOT, clicking COBTRAN, and then clicking Edit step.

link.linktext: Determining the source of generated JCL
